title: Hardware: Motherboard & Buses
subtitle: Label it, Name it, Know it!
overview: Students explore the heart of a computer — the motherboard! They learn to identify key components, ports, and buses through diagram labeling activities, real hardware observation, and hands-on port identification challenges.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — motherboard close-ups, port identification activities, student diagrams, whiteboard sketches.

keyTopics: Motherboard — The City Map connecting CPU, RAM, GPU and all peripherals
CPU Socket — Where the processor (brain) plugs in | LGA vs PGA socket types
RAM Slots — DIMM slots for short-term memory sticks
PCIe Slots (Peripheral Component Interconnect Express) — Superhighway for GPU, sound cards, Wi-Fi cards
SATA Connectors — Connect Hard Drives and SSDs for storage
Chipset — Traffic controller between CPU and peripherals (Northbridge / Southbridge)
BIOS/UEFI Chip — The startup brain that wakes the computer when you press power
CMOS Battery — Tiny watch battery that keeps time and BIOS settings alive
System Bus — Data highway between CPU, RAM, and components | 3 types: Data Bus, Address Bus, Control Bus
I/O Ports — USB, HDMI, DisplayPort, Audio Jack, Ethernet, PS/2 — all on the rear panel
Front Panel Connectors — Power button, Reset button, USB 3.0 headers
Form Factors — ATX (full size), Micro-ATX, Mini-ITX (tiny)

activity.title: Label the Motherboard Diagram + Port Identification Challenge
parts: Real or dummy motherboard for observation (optional)
Port identification card set (USB, HDMI, Ethernet, Audio, etc.)
steps: Hand out blank motherboard diagram sheets to each student
Show real motherboard photo on screen — students identify parts together
Students label: CPU Socket, RAM Slots, PCIe Slot, SATA Connector, Power Connector, BIOS Chip, CMOS Battery
Color-code each zone: CPU area (red), Memory area (blue), Storage area (green), Power area (yellow)
Port Identification Challenge: Show a port photo → students race to name it (USB-A, USB-C, HDMI, RJ45, 3.5mm Audio)
Bus Diagram Activity: Draw the 3 buses on the board — Data Bus (red arrows), Address Bus (blue arrows), Control Bus (green arrows)
Challenge: Which motherboard form factor fits in a mini PC? (Mini-ITX wins!)

quiz.q: What is the main role of a Motherboard?
options: The central circuit board connecting all computer components
quiz.explanation: Motherboard = City Map! It connects the CPU, RAM, GPU, storage and all peripherals so they can communicate.

quiz.q: Where does the CPU (processor) connect on the motherboard?
quiz.explanation: CPU Socket is where the processor plugs in. Different CPUs need different socket types — LGA (Intel) or PGA (AMD).

quiz.q: What are RAM slots used for?
options: Inserting memory (RAM) sticks for short-term storage
quiz.explanation: RAM Slots (DIMM slots) hold memory sticks. RAM is short-term memory — it stores what the computer is currently working on!
PCIe = Peripheral Component Interconnect Express — a high-speed slot used for Graphics Cards (GPU), sound cards, and Wi-Fi cards.

quiz.q: Which connector is used to attach a Hard Drive or SSD to the motherboard?
quiz.explanation: SATA (Serial ATA) connectors link Hard Drives and SSDs to the motherboard, allowing data to flow to and from storage.

quiz.q: What is the BIOS/UEFI chip responsible for?
options: Starting up the computer and checking hardware before the OS loads
quiz.explanation: BIOS/UEFI = Startup Brain! It runs first when you press power, checks all hardware, then hands control to the Operating System.

quiz.q: Why is there a CMOS battery on the motherboard?
options: To keep the BIOS settings and system clock running even when the PC is off
quiz.explanation: CMOS Battery is a tiny watch battery. Without it, your clock resets and BIOS settings vanish every time you turn the PC off!

quiz.q: What does the DATA BUS do?
options: Tells the memory which address to use
Carries the actual data/information between CPU and RAM
quiz.explanation: Data Bus = the highway carrying actual information (data bits) between the CPU and memory. More lanes (wider bus) = faster transfer!

quiz.q: What is the role of the ADDRESS BUS?
options: Carries data between CPU and hard drive
Specifies the memory location (address) where data should be read or written
Connects the monitor to the motherboard
quiz.explanation: Address Bus = the GPS! It tells the memory exactly which location (address) to store or fetch data from.

quiz.q: Which port would you use to connect a computer to a TV or monitor for video?
quiz.explanation: HDMI and DisplayPort carry high-definition video and audio from your computer to a monitor or TV. HDMI is most common!

quiz.q: What does the RJ45 port connect to?
options: A wired Ethernet network / internet cable
quiz.explanation: RJ45 = Ethernet port for wired internet connection. Wired is faster and more stable than Wi-Fi for gaming and large downloads!

quiz.q: Which motherboard form factor is the SMALLEST?
quiz.explanation: Mini-ITX is the smallest common form factor — great for tiny compact PCs! ATX is full-size, used in most desktop towers.

quiz.q: What is the Chipset's job on a motherboard?
options: Acts as a traffic controller managing data flow between CPU and all other components
quiz.explanation: Chipset = Traffic Controller! It manages how data moves between the CPU, RAM, storage, and peripherals — keeping everything organized.

quiz.q: How many types of buses are in a computer system bus?
options: 3 — Data Bus, Address Bus, and Control Bus
4 — Data, Address, Control, and Power Bus
quiz.explanation: 3 buses! Data Bus (carries information), Address Bus (carries memory location), Control Bus (carries read/write commands). They work as a team!

quiz.q: You plug in your keyboard and mouse using which type of port?
quiz.explanation: USB = Universal Serial Bus — the most common port for keyboards, mice, flash drives, and almost all external devices!
